heading: Authority to extend the duration of medical certificates
label: 7508 Authority to extend the duration of medical certificates
notes: Amendments2021—Subsec. (b). substituted “an individual or a specifically identified group of individuals” for “individual seamen or a specifically identified group of seamen”.Pub. L. 116–283
amendments: Amendments2021—Subsec. (b). substituted “an individual or a specifically identified group of individuals” for “individual seamen or a specifically identified group of seamen”.Pub. L. 116–283

children.heading: .—Granting of Extensions
children.label: a .—Granting of Extensions
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary may extend for not more than one year a medical certificate issued to an individual holding a license, merchant mariner’s document, or certificate of registry issued under chapter 71 or 73 if the Secretary determines that the extension is required to enable the Coast Guard to eliminate a backlog in processing applications for medical certificates or is in response to a national emergency or natural disaster.

children.heading: .—Manner of Extension
children.label: b .—Manner of Extension
children.text: An extension under this section may be granted to an individual or a specifically identified group of individuals.
